project: openreel-video fork — AI-generated objects for self-media video creation

features.name: Slice 0 — Repo bootstrap and architecture exploration
features.description: Fork upstream, set up dev environment (Node, pnpm, Vite), map monorepo structure, locate Three.js integration point, identify GeneratedClip plug-in surface.
features.evidence: See SLICE0_FINDINGS.md. Dev server verified at http://localhost:5173 (HTTP 200). Committed in c55a542.

features.name: Slice 1.1 — GeneratedClip type definition
features.description: Add `GeneratedClip` to `packages/core/src/graphics/types.ts` mirroring ShapeClip/SVGClip shape. Fields: source (TS string), sourceLanguage, providerId, promptHistory, paramsSchema, params, transform, keyframes, blendMode, blendOpacity. Re-export from @openreel/core.
features.evidence: Extended GraphicType union with 'generated'; added GeneratedClip interface, GeneratedClipPromptMessage, GeneratedClipSourceLanguage type alias, and DEFAULT_GENERATED_PARAMS_SCHEMA constant. Auto-exported via existing `graphics/index.ts` → `core/src/index.ts` chain (no manual re-export needed). Verified: `pnpm typecheck` clean across all 5 workspace projects (apps/web, apps/image, packages/core, packages/ui, packages/image-core); `pnpm --filter @openreel/core test:run` 176/176 tests pass in 1.22s, including 50 graphics-engine tests.

features.name: Slice 1.2 — GeneratedClip renderer integration
features.description: Extend `ThreeJSLayerRenderer` with `renderGeneratedClip(clip, time)` or create sibling `GeneratedClipRenderer` sharing the same Scene/Camera. Hook into `canvas-renderers.ts` dispatch. For Slice 1 MVP, support a hard-coded test clip (no AI yet) that draws something visible.
features.evidence: Added `renderGeneratedClip` to ThreeJSLayerRenderer (Three.js / WebGL path) and `renderGeneratedClipOnly` (Canvas 2D fast path) — both draw a colored rectangle whose fill comes from `params.color` (default `#3b82f6`). Source string is intentionally ignored at this layer (sandbox lands in feat-003). Exposed `readGeneratedClipColor` + `DEFAULT_GENERATED_CLIP_COLOR` from threejs-layer-renderer. Extended `GraphicClipUnion` and added `generated` branches in both the 3D dispatch (line ~1540) and 2D dispatch (line ~1606) inside `renderShapeClipToCanvas`. Widened `ShapeClip | SVGClip | StickerClip` literals in Preview.tsx to include GeneratedClip (`getGraphicClipDisplayBounds`, `findGraphicClipAtPoint`, and 5 other call sites). Added 5 unit tests for `readGeneratedClipColor` in `canvas-renderers.test.ts`. Verification: workspace `pnpm typecheck` clean across all 5 projects; `pnpm --filter @openreel/web test:run` 125 passed / 7 skipped (5 new tests for feat-002 all green); the single failing test (`project-store.test.ts:851` `editing templates` track ordering) is pre-existing on upstream main — verified by git-stashing feat-002 changes and reproducing the same failure (120 passed baseline -> 125 passed with feat-002 = exactly +5 new).

features.name: Slice 1.3 — Sandbox execution layer
features.description: Create `apps/web/src/objects/Sandbox.ts`. Execute generated TS source inside a Web Worker. Expose only a procedural SceneDescription protocol (rect/circle/line/text). Define message protocol: init(source) → init-ok|init-error, frame(t, params, requestId) → frame-result|frame-error.
features.evidence: Chose Web Worker + procedural SceneDescription over `new Function` with restricted globals. Reasoning: hang-isolation is the only decisive advantage of either approach for our single-user threat model, and procedural output keeps the Worker complexity much lower than passing Three.js objects across the boundary. Files: `apps/web/src/objects/SceneDescription.ts` (Shape union: rect/circle/line/text, normalized 0..1 coords, EMPTY_SCENE), `sandbox-engine.ts` (pure compileSource + runFrame returning discriminated-union Results, no Worker dependency — fully testable in jsdom), `sandbox-protocol.ts` (postMessage wire types), `sandbox-worker.ts` (thin Worker shell wiring engine to self.onmessage), `Sandbox.ts` (main-thread wrapper: init/renderFrame/getLatestScene/dispose, configurable per-call timeout, requestId multiplexing, Worker factory injection for tests), `index.ts` (barrel export). Verification: workspace `pnpm typecheck` clean across all 5 projects; `pnpm --filter @openreel/web test:run` 147 passed / 7 skipped (vs 125 baseline) = +22 new tests across sandbox-engine.test.ts (14 cases covering compile success/empty/whitespace/syntax-error/non-object/null/missing-frame, runFrame with t/params, thrown errors, malformed scenes) and Sandbox.test.ts (8 cases covering init resolve/reject, frame happy path, getLatestScene, concurrent multiplexing, engine errors surfacing, frame timeout, dispose-rejects-inflight, use-after-dispose); same pre-existing `project-store.test.ts:851` failure as feat-002. AI source contract: an expression evaluating to `{ frame(t, params): SceneDescription }`. No `import` or top-level function declarations allowed (single expression body required by `new Function` wrapping in sandbox-engine).

features.name: Slice 2.1 — AIProvider abstraction
features.description: Define `AIProvider` interface in `apps/web/src/ai/AIProvider.ts`. Methods: generate(request) → AsyncIterable<chunk>, listModels(). Common request shape (system/messages/tools/stream/temperature). Tools use JSON Schema as the lowest common denominator across providers.
features.evidence: Defined provider-neutral types in `apps/web/src/ai/types.ts` (Role, ChatMessage, ContentPart with text/tool_use/tool_result, ToolDefinition with JSON Schema inputSchema, GenerateRequest with model/system/messages/tools/forceTool/temperature/maxTokens, FinishReason, TokenUsage, the GenerateChunk discriminated union: message-start | text-delta | tool-use-start | tool-use-input-delta | tool-use-end | done | error, FinalResult assembly type, FinalToolCall, ModelInfo, ProviderInfo). `AIProvider.ts` defines the interface: info, listModels(), generate(request, options?) -> AsyncIterable<GenerateChunk>, plus GenerateOptions with AbortSignal. Two pure helpers: `streamToFinal` (consume AsyncIterable into FinalResult, assembles concatenated text + ordered tool calls with JSON parsing, throws StreamErrorWithPartialResult on error chunks while preserving partial), `validateGenerateRequest` (runtime shape check returning array of human-readable error strings — empty == valid). `index.ts` barrel exports the public surface. Verification: workspace `pnpm typecheck` clean across all 5 projects; `pnpm --filter @openreel/web test:run` 171 passed / 7 skipped (vs 147 baseline = +24 new tests, all green: streamToFinal.test.ts 8 cases for text/tool-call/concurrent-tools/parse-error/empty-input/usage/error/post-done, validateGenerateRequest.test.ts 16 cases covering model/messages/tools/forceTool/temperature/maxTokens edges). Same pre-existing project-store.test.ts:851 failure as before.

features.name: Slice 2.2 — Provider implementations (Claude + DeepSeek, OpenAI/Compatible deferred)
features.description: Implement ClaudeProvider (anthropic SDK + prompt caching) and DeepSeekProvider (openai SDK + baseURL override to api.deepseek.com). Provider registry with auto-default and switch semantics. OpenAIProvider and CompatibleProvider deferred to a later feat — the DeepSeek implementation already exercises the OpenAI-compatible code path so adding them is a thin re-skin. IndexedDB persistence + encrypted key storage deferred to feat-006 (settings UI) — for now, dev-time keys flow through .env.local.
features.evidence: Installed @anthropic-ai/sdk ^0.96.0 + openai ^6.37.0 via npmmirror. New files in apps/web/src/ai/providers/: openai-translation.ts (pure async generator translating OpenAI ChatCompletionChunk stream to GenerateChunk; handles delta.content, tool_calls with index/id/function fields, finish_reason mapping, trailing usage chunks; emits synthetic tool-use-end since OpenAI has no explicit marker), anthropic-translation.ts (pure async generator translating Anthropic MessageStreamEvent to GenerateChunk; tracks content_block index to toolCallId, captures cache token usage, defensively emits done if message_stop missing), DeepSeekProvider.ts (uses openai SDK with baseURL https://api.deepseek.com/v1, lists deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner models, dangerouslyAllowBrowser, request body translation including assistant tool_calls and role=tool messages), ClaudeProvider.ts (lists claude-opus-4-7 / sonnet-4-6 / haiku-4-5, enables ephemeral prompt caching on system + last tool by default with togglable enablePromptCaching, translates assistant messages with tool_use parts and tool-role messages with tool_result blocks), registry.ts (ProviderRegistry: register/unregister/get/list/setCurrent/getCurrent/getCurrentId, auto-selects first registered as current, unregister of current moves to another or clears), updated apps/web/.env.example with VITE_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / VITE_OPENAI_API_KEY / VITE_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY / VITE_COMPATIBLE_* . Tests use vi.fn() mocked SDK clients via clientFactory injection — no real network calls. Verification: workspace pnpm typecheck clean across all 5 projects; pnpm --filter @openreel/web test:run -> 213 passed / 7 skipped (vs 171 baseline = +42 new tests across 5 test files: openai-translation.test.ts 7 cases, anthropic-translation.test.ts 6 cases, DeepSeekProvider.test.ts 11 cases including body translation + retryable error classification, ClaudeProvider.test.ts 10 cases including cache_control placement + force tool choice, registry.test.ts 8 cases). Same pre-existing project-store.test.ts:851 failure as before.

features.name: Slice 2.3 — AI Panel UI
features.description: Create `apps/web/src/components/AIPanel/`. Provider+model dropdown, prompt input, generate button, streaming output area, 'Fix with AI' on render error. Settings entry for API keys (stored encrypted in IndexedDB via `keyStore.ts`). Wire createGeneratedClip into project store.

features.name: Slice 3 — Visual parameter editing (JsonSchema → form)
features.description: Component `apps/web/src/components/ParamPanel/` reads a GeneratedClip's `paramsSchema` and renders inputs (color picker, number slider, vector, select, text). Param changes flow back to clip without re-running AI. Params support keyframe binding via the existing AnimationEngine.

features.name: Slice 4 — Monaco source editor
features.description: Component `apps/web/src/components/SourceEditor/` opens on double-click of a GeneratedClip and shows its `source`. Save re-mounts the clip's sandbox with new source. 'Ask AI to refactor' button: send selection + instruction to provider, replace selection with response.

features.name: Slice 5 — Action/state system
features.description: Allow each GeneratedClip to define named states (idle/walk/jump etc.) as parameter presets. Timeline can place 'state change' markers that trigger smooth transitions of params between states. Built on top of existing keyframe machinery, no new core engine.

features.name: Slice 6 — Object template library + E2E verification
features.description: Bundled object templates (stick figure, geometric shapes, text bubble, emoji character). User can pick a template and ask AI to mutate it. End-to-end smoke: prompt → generate → add to timeline → tweak params → export mp4, with playback matching preview at 1080p.

policies.additive_only: New code lives in src/ai/, src/objects/, src/components/AIPanel|ParamPanel|SourceEditor/. Existing openreel files only modified if the feature strictly requires it.
